description:
## Background
The App currently performs its initial Issue Explorer load and Workspace state refresh inside a direct React `useEffect`. The no-use-effect policy must cover this effect without changing startup ordering or failure recovery.

## What to build
Replace the App startup effect that loads Issue Explorer state and refreshes Workspace state on initial mount. Keep the existing startup transition-gate decisions, load-failure state, and workspace refresh behavior while removing the direct `useEffect` call.

## Technical Approach
Choose the replacement that makes the one-time external synchronization explicit, such as the project `useMountEffect` escape hatch or an equivalent mount boundary. Keep the asynchronous load and refresh operations coordinated with the existing transition gate, and preserve the current dependency on the refresh callback without introducing a state relay.

## Considerations
The initial load can resolve after a Workspace transition has been accepted, so generation ordering and ignored decisions must remain intact. Transport failures must continue to leave the existing failure state rather than throwing during render.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The initial Issue Explorer load and Workspace refresh still run once when App mounts.
- [ ] Startup success, transport failure, and transition-generation behavior are unchanged.
- [ ] This effect has no direct `useEffect` call and passes the configured Oxlint restriction.
- [ ] Focused tests and the relevant typecheck/lint commands pass.

## Test Plan
- Run existing App/startup transition tests.
- Add or update a regression test if the chosen lifecycle boundary changes observable timing.
- Run the relevant Oxlint and TypeScript checks.

Triage: ready-for-agent.
title: Replace App initial issue/workspace load effect
